title: Pakistan opens Kartarpur Corridor for Sikh pilgrims
description: Pakistan completed the Kartarpur Corridor allowing visa-free access for Indian Sikh pilgrims to Gurdwara Darbar Sahib, where Guru Nanak died in 1539. Prime Minister Imran Khan inaugurated the corridor as a gesture of religious tolerance and peace diplomacy.
outcome: The corridor eased Pakistan-India tensions temporarily and showcased Muslim-majority Pakistan's protection of non-Muslim heritage sites.

title: Iran protests following Mahsa Amini's death
description: Mahsa Amini, a 22-year-old Kurdish woman, died in custody of Iran's morality police after arrest for allegedly violating hijab rules. Her death sparked nationwide protests with the slogan 'Woman, Life, Freedom' challenging the Islamic Republic's mandatory veiling laws.
outcome: The protests represented the most serious challenge to Iran's theocratic system in years, with particular participation from women and youth.

title: FIFA World Cup held in Muslim country for first time
description: Qatar hosted the FIFA World Cup, the first Muslim-majority country and first Middle Eastern nation to do so. The tournament sparked debates about migrant worker conditions, alcohol restrictions, and LGBTQ rights in Islamic contexts.
outcome: The World Cup showcased Qatari infrastructure investment while intensifying global scrutiny of Gulf labor systems and Islamic law in international events.

title: Turkey and Syria earthquakes devastate Muslim communities
description: Massive earthquakes measuring 7.8 and 7.5 magnitude struck southern Turkey and northern Syria in February, killing over 50,000 people. The disaster particularly affected Syrian refugees and Turkish cities like Gaziantep and Kahramanmaraş.
outcome: The earthquakes caused one of the century's deadliest natural disasters, exposing building code violations and complicating Syrian conflict recovery.

title: Indonesia completes presidential election with peaceful transfer
description: Prabowo Subianto won Indonesia's presidential election, succeeding Joko Widodo in the world's largest Muslim-majority democracy. The peaceful democratic transition continued Indonesia's two-decade pattern of stable electoral politics following the end of authoritarian rule.
outcome: The election demonstrated the stability of Indonesian democracy and moderate Islam's compatibility with democratic governance.

title: Karzai elected interim leader of Afghanistan
description: Hamid Karzai was selected by the Bonn Conference to lead Afghanistan's interim administration following the fall of the Taliban. He became the first democratically elected president of Afghanistan in 2004.
outcome: Karzai's leadership marked Afghanistan's transition to post-Taliban governance under international oversight.

title: Death of Ahmad Yasin, Hamas founder
description: Sheikh Ahmad Yasin, founder and spiritual leader of Hamas, was assassinated by Israeli forces in a targeted airstrike in Gaza. His death sparked widespread protests across the Muslim world.
outcome: Yasin's assassination intensified Palestinian resistance movements and Hamas political consolidation.

title: Madrid train bombings by al-Qaeda-inspired cell
description: Ten coordinated bombings on commuter trains in Madrid killed 193 people and wounded over 2,000. The attacks were carried out by an Islamist militant cell inspired by al-Qaeda, leading to Spain's withdrawal from Iraq.
outcome: The attacks influenced Spanish elections and led to the withdrawal of Spanish troops from Iraq.

title: Mahmoud Abbas elected President of Palestinian Authority
description: Mahmoud Abbas won the Palestinian presidential election following Yasser Arafat's death, becoming leader of the Palestinian Authority. He represented a more moderate stance than his predecessor.
outcome: Abbas's election marked a shift toward renewed peace negotiations with Israel.

title: Hamas wins Palestinian legislative elections
description: Hamas won a majority in the Palestinian Legislative Council elections, defeating Fatah in a democratic vote. The victory led to international sanctions and eventual Hamas control of Gaza.
outcome: Hamas's electoral victory deepened Palestinian political divisions and led to the Gaza-West Bank split.

title: Benazir Bhutto assassinated in Pakistan
description: Former Pakistani Prime Minister Benazir Bhutto was assassinated in Rawalpindi during an election campaign rally. Her death shocked the Muslim world and destabilized Pakistani politics.
outcome: Bhutto's assassination eliminated a major secular political figure and deepened Pakistan's instability.

title: Turkey lifts headscarf ban in universities
description: Turkish parliament voted to lift the decades-old ban on headscarves in universities, a major victory for religious freedoms. The reform was championed by the AKP government under Prime Minister Erdoğan.
outcome: The reform represented a significant shift away from Kemalist secularism toward greater religious expression.

title: Death of Grand Ayatollah Montazeri
description: Grand Ayatollah Hossein-Ali Montazeri, once designated successor to Khomeini and later a dissident critic of the Iranian regime, died in Qom. His funeral drew massive crowds supporting reform.
outcome: Montazeri's death galvanized the Green Movement and highlighted internal clerical opposition to the regime.

title: Floods devastate Pakistan, killing thousands
description: Catastrophic monsoon floods affected one-fifth of Pakistan's territory, killing nearly 2,000 people and displacing 20 million. The disaster prompted massive international humanitarian response.
outcome: The floods represented one of the worst natural disasters in Pakistani history and exposed governmental inadequacies.

title: Mohammed Morsi elected President of Egypt
description: Mohammed Morsi of the Muslim Brotherhood became Egypt's first democratically elected president following the Arab Spring. His presidency lasted only one year before military intervention.
outcome: Morsi's brief presidency represented the only democratic transition in Egypt before military restoration.

title: Gezi Park protests in Turkey
description: Protests against urban development in Istanbul's Gezi Park expanded into nationwide demonstrations against Erdoğan's government. The movement drew diverse opposition including secularists, Kurds, and liberal Muslims.
outcome: The protests marked a turning point toward more authoritarian governance in Turkey.

title: Abduction of Chibok schoolgirls by Boko Haram
description: Boko Haram militants abducted 276 female students from a school in Chibok, Nigeria, sparking global outrage and the #BringBackOurGirls campaign. Many remained in captivity for years.
outcome: The abduction highlighted Boko Haram's campaign of terror and the vulnerability of education in conflict zones.

title: Sisi opens New Suez Canal expansion
description: Egyptian President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi inaugurated a major expansion of the Suez Canal, allowing two-way traffic and reducing transit time. The project was completed in one year.
outcome: The expansion enhanced Egypt's strategic economic importance and maritime trade capacity.

title: Death of Muhammad Ali, global Muslim icon
description: Boxing legend Muhammad Ali died in Arizona, remembered globally as both a sports icon and advocate for Black Muslim identity and civil rights. His funeral in Louisville drew world leaders.
outcome: Ali's death prompted global reflection on Islam in America and Black Muslim heritage.

title: Battle of Marawi in the Philippines
description: ISIS-affiliated militants seized Marawi City in the southern Philippines, leading to a five-month siege and urban warfare. Philippine forces eventually retook the city with heavy destruction.
outcome: The siege demonstrated ISIS's reach into Southeast Asian Muslim communities and left Marawi devastated.

title: Mahathir Mohamad returns as Malaysian PM at 92
description: Mahathir Mohamad became the world's oldest head of government, returning as Malaysia's Prime Minister after leading opposition to defeat his former party in historic elections. He had previously served from 1981-2003.
outcome: Mahathir's return marked Malaysia's first democratic transfer of power since independence.

title: India revokes Kashmir's special status
description: The Indian government under Narendra Modi revoked Article 370, ending Jammu and Kashmir's special autonomous status and imposing a military lockdown. The move sparked international concern over the Muslim-majority region.
outcome: The revocation fundamentally altered Kashmir's political status and intensified tensions with Pakistan.

title: Grand Mosque scaled to minimal Hajj pilgrims
description: Saudi Arabia limited Hajj to only 10,000 domestic pilgrims due to COVID-19 pandemic, down from over 2 million annually. This marked the most restricted Hajj in modern history.
outcome: The restrictions demonstrated unprecedented pandemic adaptation in Islamic pilgrimage practices.

title: Ebrahim Raisi elected President of Iran
description: Hardliner Ebrahim Raisi won Iran's presidential election amid low turnout and disqualification of reform candidates. His election consolidated conservative control across Iranian institutions.
outcome: Raisi's presidency ended any pretense of reform and aligned executive with supreme leader's hardline policies.

title: Louvre Abu Dhabi acquires major Islamic art collection
description: The Louvre Abu Dhabi acquired a significant collection of Islamic manuscripts and artifacts, enhancing its position as a major center for Islamic art preservation. The museum continued its mission of cultural bridge-building.
outcome: The acquisition strengthened the UAE's cultural diplomacy and Islamic heritage preservation efforts.

title: Death of Ebrahim Raisi in helicopter crash
description: Iranian President Ebrahim Raisi died in a helicopter crash in mountainous terrain near the Azerbaijan border, along with Foreign Minister Hossein Amir-Abdollahian. The incident sparked succession questions in Iran.
outcome: Raisi's death created a leadership transition during a period of regional tension and domestic unrest.

title: Bangladesh's Sheikh Hasina flees amid mass protests
description: Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina fled to India after weeks of violent protests over job quotas evolved into demands for her resignation. Student-led movements toppled her 15-year rule.
outcome: Hasina's fall marked a dramatic shift in Bangladesh's political landscape after decades of Awami League dominance.

title: Indonesia inaugurates new capital Nusantara
description: Indonesia officially relocated its capital from Jakarta to the new purpose-built city of Nusantara in East Kalimantan. President Joko Widodo led the historic move affecting the world's largest Muslim-majority nation.
outcome: The new capital represented Indonesia's ambitious vision for decentralization and sustainable urban development.

title: Opening of Grand Egyptian Museum
description: Egypt fully opened the Grand Egyptian Museum near the Giza Pyramids, the world's largest archaeological museum. The facility showcases pharaonic heritage alongside Islamic Egyptian civilization exhibits.
outcome: The museum's opening marked a major milestone in Egypt's cultural tourism and heritage preservation strategy.

title: Senegal elects Bassirou Diomaye Faye as youngest African leader
description: Bassirou Diomaye Faye, 44, won Senegal's presidential election as an anti-establishment candidate, becoming Africa's youngest elected head of state. His victory represented a generational shift in West African Muslim politics.
outcome: Faye's election demonstrated youth political mobilization and democratic resilience in Muslim West Africa.

title: Karzai elected interim leader of Afghanistan
description: Hamid Karzai was selected by the Bonn Conference to lead Afghanistan's interim administration following the fall of the Taliban. He became the first democratically elected president in 2004, serving until 2014.
outcome: Karzai's leadership marked Afghanistan's transition toward democratic governance after decades of conflict.

title: Death of Ahmad Yasin, Hamas founder
description: Sheikh Ahmad Yasin, the wheelchair-bound founder and spiritual leader of Hamas, was killed by an Israeli helicopter strike in Gaza City. His assassination sparked widespread protests across the Muslim world.
outcome: Yasin's death elevated him to martyrdom status among Palestinians and strengthened Hamas's resolve.

title: Madrid train bombings by al-Qaeda-inspired cell
description: Coordinated bombings on Madrid commuter trains killed 193 people and wounded over 2,000, carried out by an Islamist militant cell. The attacks occurred three days before Spanish elections and led to Spain's withdrawal from Iraq.
outcome: The bombings became Europe's deadliest terrorist attack since Lockerbie and altered Spanish foreign policy.

title: Mahmoud Abbas elected President of Palestinian Authority
description: Mahmoud Abbas won the Palestinian presidential election following Yasser Arafat's death, gaining 62% of the vote. He pledged to pursue peace negotiations with Israel while maintaining Palestinian national rights.
outcome: Abbas's election provided continuity for Palestinian leadership but faced challenges from Hamas's growing popularity.

title: Hamas wins Palestinian legislative elections
description: Hamas won a majority in Palestinian Legislative Council elections, defeating Fatah and shocking international observers. The victory led to Western sanctions and eventual violent split with Fatah.
outcome: The election deepened Palestinian political division and led to Hamas's control of Gaza from 2007.

title: Benazir Bhutto assassinated in Pakistan
description: Former Prime Minister Benazir Bhutto was killed in a gun and bomb attack after an election rally in Rawalpindi. She was the first woman to lead a Muslim-majority nation and had returned from exile to contest elections.
sources: United Nations Commission of Inquiry Report 2010
outcome: Bhutto's assassination shocked the Muslim world and destabilized Pakistan's democratic transition.

title: Turkey lifts headscarf ban in universities
description: Turkey's parliament voted to lift the decades-old ban on headscarves in universities, a key reform by the AKP government. The move reversed Kemalist restrictions and sparked debate about secularism and religious freedom.
outcome: The reform marked a significant shift in Turkish secularism and expanded religious expression in public institutions.

title: Death of Grand Ayatollah Montazeri
description: Grand Ayatollah Hossein-Ali Montazeri, once designated successor to Khomeini and later dissident critic of Iran's government, died under house arrest. He had challenged the concept of velayat-e faqih and defended human rights.
outcome: Montazeri's death was mourned by reformists and highlighted tensions between clerical authority and democratic governance in Iran.

title: Floods devastate Pakistan, killing thousands
description: Catastrophic monsoon floods affected over 20 million Pakistanis, killing nearly 2,000 and destroying vast agricultural land. The disaster became one of the worst humanitarian crises in Pakistan's history.
outcome: The floods exposed governance failures and triggered massive international humanitarian response.

title: Mohammed Morsi elected President of Egypt
description: Muslim Brotherhood candidate Mohammed Morsi won Egypt's first free presidential election, defeating Ahmed Shafiq. His victory represented the Arab Spring's democratic promise but ended with military coup in 2013.
outcome: Morsi's brief presidency highlighted tensions between Islamist governance and military power in Egypt.

title: Gezi Park protests in Turkey
description: Mass protests erupted in Istanbul against government plans to demolish Gezi Park, evolving into nationwide demonstrations against Erdoğan's AKP government. Millions participated in what became Turkey's largest protests in decades.
outcome: The protests marked a turning point in Turkish politics, revealing deep divisions over secularism and authoritarianism.

title: Abduction of Chibok schoolgirls by Boko Haram
description: Boko Haram militants abducted 276 female students from a government secondary school in Chibok, Nigeria, sparking global #BringBackOurGirls campaign. Many remain missing or were forced into marriages.
outcome: The mass kidnapping exposed Boko Haram's brutality and Nigeria's security failures, galvanizing international attention.

title: Sisi opens New Suez Canal expansion
description: Egyptian President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi inaugurated the New Suez Canal expansion, adding a 35-kilometer parallel waterway to increase traffic capacity. The project was completed in one year as a nationalist achievement.
outcome: The expansion bolstered Egypt's economy and reinforced the canal's strategic importance to global maritime trade.

title: India revokes Kashmir's special status
description: India's BJP government revoked Article 370 of the Constitution, ending Jammu and Kashmir's autonomy and statehood. The Muslim-majority region was placed under lockdown, sparking international concern and Pakistani protests.
outcome: The revocation fundamentally altered Kashmir's status and intensified tensions between India and Pakistan.
